task_completion.description: The flower image is opened in an image editing application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A flower image is visible within the workspace of a graphical application like GIMP.

task_completion.description: The agent selects the tool or menu option to flip the image vertically.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A menu showing a vertical flip option (e.g., Image -> Transform -> Flip Vertically) is open, or the Flip tool is selected and set to 'Vertical' in the tool options.

task_completion.description: The flower image is successfully flipped vertically on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas appears upside down (top and bottom are swapped). Crucially, the left and right sides must remain in their original positions (it should not be rotated 180 degrees).

task_completion.description: The agent initiates saving or exporting the flipped image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export Image' dialog box is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The flipped image is saved or exported to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The export/save dialog shows the destination as the Desktop with an appropriate filename (e.g., 'flower_vertical_mirror'), or a new image file icon appears on the Desktop background.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI image editor to perform the task rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image manipulation happens within a graphical application interface (like GIMP), and no terminal commands (like ImageMagick's 'convert') are used to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The image was flipped vertically, not rotated 180 degrees.
integrity.visual_evidence: The left and right elements of the image remain on their respective sides; only the top and bottom are inverted. A 180-degree rotation would incorrectly swap both top/bottom AND left/right.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved the file through the application's interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save/export dialog is interacted with and completed, rather than the agent just moving a pre-existing file or using terminal commands to create the output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires a pure vertical flip (top-to-bottom mirror), not a horizontal flip or a 180-degree rotation. A successful vertical flip preserves the left-right orientation while swapping top and bottom. The environment is GIMP-based. The verifier expects the output file to be saved on the Desktop with a name like 'flower_vertical_mirror' (or similar accepted names like 'vertical_mirror', 'flower_image_vertical', 'flower_flipped') and a standard image extension (.png, .jpg, or .jpeg).
